message.content:
Research creating a simple Tauri app for mouse jiggling on macOS. The goal is to have a native macOS app that:

1. Can be granted Accessibility permissions as its own app
2. Moves the mouse slightly to prevent idle detection
3. Runs in the background (menu bar app ideally)

Explore:
1. How to create a basic Tauri app with `create-tauri-app`
2. Rust crates for mouse automation on macOS (enigo, autopilot-rs, core-graphics, etc.)
3. How to make it a menu bar / system tray app
4. macOS-specific considerations for Accessibility permissions

Focus on the simplest viable implementation.

content.text: I'll research how to create a Tauri app for mouse jiggling on macOS. Let me gather comprehensive information about this.

input.query: Tauri macOS mouse automation accessibility permissions
Rust crates mouse control macOS enigo autopilot
Tauri system tray menu bar macOS
macOS accessibility permissions Tauri native app

content.content:
Web search results for query: "Rust crates mouse control macOS enigo autopilot"

Links: [{"title":"autopilot - crates.io: Rust Package Registry","url":"https://crates.io/crates/autopilot/"},{"title":"enigo - crates.io: Rust Package Registry","url":"https://crates.io/crates/enigo"},{"title":"AutoPilot — Rust API for macOS/iOS","url":"https://lib.rs/crates/autopilot"},{"title":"Enigo — Rust HW library // Lib.rs","url":"https://lib.rs/crates/enigo"},{"title":"enigo - Rust","url":"https://docs.rs/enigo/"},{"title":"enigo-copy - crates.io: Rust Package Registry","url":"https://crates.io/crates/enigo-copy"},{"title":"GitHub - enigo-rs/enigo: Cross platform input simulation in Rust","url":"https://github.com/enigo-rs/enigo"},{"title":"GitHub - gmasse/macmatic: A Rust library for macOS automation with image detection, keyboard and mouse control.","url":"https://github.com/gmasse/macmatic"},{"title":"mouse-rs - crates.io: Rust Package Registry","url":"https://crates.io/crates/mouse-rs"},{"title":"GitHub - autopilot-rs/autopilot-rs: A simple, cross-platform GUI automation module for Rust.","url":"https://github.com/autopilot-rs/autopilot-rs"}]

## Search Results

I found several Rust crates for mouse control on macOS:

### **Enigo**
A cross-platform (Linux, Windows, macOS & BSD) library to simulate keyboard and mouse events. You can use it to move the mouse, perform button clicks, and enter text. By default, enigo currently works on Windows, macOS and Linux (X11).

### **AutoPilot**
AutoPilot is a Rust port of the Python C extension AutoPy, a simple, cross-platform GUI automation library for Python. Currently supported on macOS, Windows, and X11 with the XTest extension.

### **Mouse-rs**
Mouse-rs is a rust library for controlling your mouse from a rust program, without having to go into your kernel yourself.

Both **Enigo** and **AutoPilot** are popular choices for macOS mouse control, with Enigo being more actively maintained and feature-rich with support for keyboard input as well.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

### **Enigo**
A cross-platform (Linux, Windows, macOS & BSD) library to simulate keyboard and mouse events. You can use it to move the mouse, perform button clicks, and enter text. By default, enigo currently works on Windows, macOS and Linux (X11).

### **AutoPilot**
AutoPilot is a Rust port of the Python C extension AutoPy, a simple, cross-platform GUI automation library for Python. Currently supported on macOS, Windows, and X11 with the XTest extension.

### **Mouse-rs**
Mouse-rs is a rust library for controlling your mouse from a rust program, without having to go into your kernel yourself.

Both **Enigo** and **AutoPilot** are popular choices for macOS mouse control, with Enigo being more actively maintained and feature-rich with support for keyboard input as well.

Links: [{"title":"GitHub - ayangweb/tauri-plugin-macos-permissions: Support for checking and requesting macos system permissions.","url":"https://github.com/ayangweb/tauri-plugin-macos-permissions"},{"title":"Permissions | Tauri","url":"https://v2.tauri.app/security/permissions/"},{"title":"tauri-plugin-macos-permissions 2.0.3 - Docs.rs","url":"https://docs.rs/crate/tauri-plugin-macos-permissions/2.0.3"},{"title":"tauri-plugin-macos-permissions - crates.io: Rust Package Registry","url":"https://crates.io/crates/tauri-plugin-macos-permissions"},{"title":"[bug][v1] Re-granting macOS permissions after every app update · Issue #11085 · tauri-apps/tauri","url":"https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri/issues/11085"},{"title":"Permission | Tauri","url":"https://tauri.app/reference/acl/permission/"},{"title":"Core Permissions | Tauri","url":"https://tauri.app/reference/acl/core-permissions/"},{"title":"tauri-plugin-macos-permissions — system library interface for Rust // Lib.rs","url":"https://lib.rs/crates/tauri-plugin-macos-permissions"},{"title":"Capabilities | Tauri","url":"https://v2.tauri.app/security/capabilities/"},{"title":"tauri-plugin-macos-permissions 2.3.0 - Docs.rs","url":"https://docs.rs/crate/tauri-plugin-macos-permissions/latest"}]

I've completed the web search for "Tauri macOS mouse automation accessibility permissions". Here are the key findings:

## Primary Resource

The main resource for this topic is the tauri-plugin-macos-permissions plugin, which uses macos-accessibility-client to check and request accessibility permission.

## Functionality

The plugin supports requesting input monitoring, and includes methods to check input monitoring permission and request input monitoring permission. This would cover mouse automation capabilities on macOS.

## Plugin Details

The plugin allows checking and requesting macOS permissions to accessibility and full disk access. The plugin only works on Tauri v2.

## Integration

To use it in a Tauri application, you need to add "macos-permissions:default" to the permissions array in src-tauri/capabilities/default.json.

The search results show that this is the recommended approach for handling macOS accessibility and input monitoring permissions within Tauri applications, which would be necessary for mouse automation features.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Links: [{"title":"GitHub - ayangweb/tauri-plugin-macos-permissions: Support for checking and requesting macos system permissions.","url":"https://github.com/ayangweb/tauri-plugin-macos-permissions"},{"title":"tauri-plugin-macos-permissions - crates.io: Rust Package Registry","url":"https://crates.io/crates/tauri-plugin-macos-permissions"},{"title":"tauri-plugin-macos-permissions 2.0.3 - Docs.rs","url":"https://docs.rs/crate/tauri-plugin-macos-permissions/2.0.3"},{"title":"Capabilities | Tauri","url":"https://v2.tauri.app/security/capabilities/"},{"title":"[bug][v1] Re-granting macOS permissions after every app update · Issue #11085 · tauri-apps/tauri","url":"https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri/issues/11085"},{"title":"Permissions | Tauri","url":"https://v2.tauri.app/security/permissions/"},{"title":"tauri-plugin-macos-permissions — system library interface for Rust // Lib.rs","url":"https://lib.rs/crates/tauri-plugin-macos-permissions"},{"title":"Permission | Tauri","url":"https://tauri.app/reference/acl/permission/"},{"title":"tauri-plugin-macos-permissions 2.3.0 - Docs.rs","url":"https://docs.rs/crate/tauri-plugin-macos-permissions/latest"},{"title":"Core Permissions | Tauri","url":"https://v2.tauri.app/reference/acl/core-permissions/"}]

Here are the search results for macOS accessibility permissions with Tauri native apps:

## Key Findings

The `tauri-plugin-macos-permissions` plugin provides support for checking and requesting macOS system permissions. This is a popular community plugin for handling accessibility and other system permissions in Tauri applications.

### Plugin Features

The plugin can check and request macOS permissions to accessibility and full disk access. Additionally, it uses `macos-accessibility-client` to check and request accessibility permission, `FullDiskAccess` to check full disk access permission, and `objc2` to check and request microphone or camera permissions.

### Usage

To use this plugin in a Tauri app, you can:

1. Add the plugin to your Rust code by calling `.plugin(tauri_plugin_macos_permissions::init())` in your Tauri builder, and then access the API through JavaScript guest bindings like `import { checkAccessibilityPermissions } from "tauri-plugin-macos-permissions-api"`.

2. Tauri provides a capabilities system to granularly enable and constrain the core exposure to the application frontend, with capabilities defining which permissions are granted or denied for which windows or webviews.

The plugin is available on crates.io and GitHub at [ayangweb/tauri-plugin-macos-permissions](https://github.com/ayangweb/tauri-plugin-macos-permissions).


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Links: [{"title":"Window Menu | Tauri","url":"https://v2.tauri.app/learn/window-menu/"},{"title":"System Tray | Tauri v1","url":"https://v1.tauri.app/v1/guides/features/system-tray/"},{"title":"GitHub - ahkohd/tauri-macos-menubar-app-example: An example macOS Menubar app built with Tauri","url":"https://github.com/ahkohd/tauri-macos-menubar-app-example"},{"title":"System Tray | Tauri","url":"https://v2.tauri.app/learn/system-tray/"},{"title":"GitHub - 4gray/tauri-menubar-app: a simple menubar application based on tauri framework","url":"https://github.com/4gray/tauri-menubar-app"},{"title":"System Tray - The Tauri Documentation WIP","url":"https://jonaskruckenberg.github.io/tauri-docs-wip/examples/system-tray.html"},{"title":"[bug] macOS Tray Icon does not appear · Issue #13770 · tauri-apps/tauri","url":"https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri/issues/13770"},{"title":"menu | Tauri","url":"https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemenu/"},{"title":"Understanding the System Tray: From Concept to Tauri v2 Implementation | by Sjobeiri | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@sjobeiri/understanding-the-system-tray-from-concept-to-tauri-v2-implementation-252f278bb57c"},{"title":"tray | Tauri","url":"https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacetray/"}]

I found comprehensive information about Tauri system tray and menu bar functionality for macOS. Here are the key findings:

## System Tray on macOS

On macOS, the Tray will be located on the top right corner of your screen, adjacent to your battery and wifi icons.(no content)On macOS, similar functionality appears in the menu bar, where small icons live next to the clock and system menu.

## Core Features

Native application menus can be attached to both to a window or system tray.(no content)Tray icons have their own context menu.

## Menu Bar on macOS

Multi-level menus will appear as part of the application window for Windows or Linux, or in the menu bar on MacOS. However, there are some specific requirements: When using submenus on MacOS, all items must be grouped under a submenu. Top-level items will be ignored.

## Configuration

To set up a system tray in your `tauri.conf.json`, you need to configure the `systemTray` object with icon settings. iconAsTemplate is a boolean value that determines whether the image represents a Template Image on macOS.

## Click Handling

By default the menu is displayed on both left and right clicks. To prevent the menu from popping up on left click, call the menu_on_left_click(false) Rust function or set the menuOnLeftClick JavaScript option to false.

The search results include references to official Tauri documentation and example projects for building menubar applications with Tauri on macOS.

I found comprehensive information about Tauri system tray and menu bar functionality for macOS. Here are the key findings:

## System Tray on macOS

On macOS, the Tray will be located on the top right corner of your screen, adjacent to your battery and wifi icons.(no content)On macOS, similar functionality appears in the menu bar, where small icons live next to the clock and system menu.

## Core Features

Native application menus can be attached to both to a window or system tray.(no content)Tray icons have their own context menu.

## Menu Bar on macOS

Multi-level menus will appear as part of the application window for Windows or Linux, or in the menu bar on MacOS. However, there are some specific requirements: When using submenus on MacOS, all items must be grouped under a submenu. Top-level items will be ignored.

## Configuration

To set up a system tray in your `tauri.conf.json`, you need to configure the `systemTray` object with icon settings. iconAsTemplate is a boolean value that determines whether the image represents a Template Image on macOS.

## Click Handling

By default the menu is displayed on both left and right clicks. To prevent the menu from popping up on left click, call the menu_on_left_click(false) Rust function or set the menuOnLeftClick JavaScript option to false.

Links: [{"title":"Create a Project | Tauri","url":"https://v2.tauri.app/start/create-project/"},{"title":"GitHub - tauri-apps/create-tauri-app: Rapidly scaffold out a new tauri app project.","url":"https://github.com/tauri-apps/create-tauri-app"},{"title":"Develop | Tauri","url":"https://v2.tauri.app/develop/"},{"title":"What is Tauri? | Tauri","url":"https://v2.tauri.app/start/"},{"title":"Prerequisites | Tauri","url":"https://v2.tauri.app/start/prerequisites/"},{"title":"Quick Start | Tauri v1","url":"https://v1.tauri.app/v1/guides/getting-started/setup/"},{"title":"Creating Your First Tauri App with React: A Beginner's Guide - DEV Community","url":"https://dev.to/dubisdev/creating-your-first-tauri-app-with-react-a-beginners-guide-3eb2"},{"title":"Making Mobile Apps at the Speed of Thought with Tauri // Erik Horton","url":"https://blog.erikhorton.com/2025/08/10/making-mobile-apps-at-the-speed-of-thought-with-tauri.html"},{"title":"GitHub - tauri-apps/tauri: Build smaller, faster, and more secure desktop and mobile applications with a web frontend.","url":"https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri"},{"title":"Build Web Desktop Apps using Tauri | by Jessish Pothancheri | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@kaljessy/build-web-desktop-apps-using-tauri-6fe12586016a"}]

Based on the search results, here's a comprehensive overview of the create-tauri-app setup guide for 2025:

## Getting Started with create-tauri-app

To get started using create-tauri-app run one of the below commands in the folder you'd like to setup your project.

### Prerequisites

Before setting up your Tauri project, be sure to follow the prerequisites guide to install all of the dependencies required by Tauri. Key requirements include:

- Tauri is built with Rust and requires it for development. Install Rust using one of following methods.
- Node.js (for package management)
- Platform-specific dependencies (varies by OS)

### Project Creation

The easiest way to get started is to use cargo create-tauri-app and follow the prompts. You're going to get set up with a Rust backend and one of a few frontend choices.(no content)Follow along with the prompts to choose your project name, frontend language, package manager, and frontend framework, and frontend framework options if applicable.

### Available Frontend Options

You can use plain HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, or any framework you prefer such as Next.js, Nuxt, Svelte, Yew, or Leptos.

### Project Structure

From here, you'll be building your backend inside src-tauri/src/ and your frontend inside the src directory. Tauri will handle the bundling and communication between the two through the use of commands.

### Running Your App

Verify your Tauri app is working by running the development server: This command will compile the Rust code and open a window with your web content.

### Mobile Development

Projects created with create-tauri-app configures your development server for mobile dev out of the box.
